title: The Fir & The Bramble
content:
The Fir-tree looked down on the Bramble.
    "Poor thing, only able to scramble
    About on the ground."
    Just then an axe' sound
    Made the Fir wish himself but a Bramble.

    Pride Of Place Has Its Disadvantages
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Walter Crane delivers a powerful performance in "The Fir & The Bramble"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
